detail_page.section_name: Hero: Scheme Overview & Call to Action
detail_page.ui_component: Banner with large name, short title, level/state/benefit type/DBT badges, implementing agency name, prominent 'Apply Now' button. Scheme image as background or alongside if available.
detail_page.notes: First impression. Clear name, concise value, immediate call to action. Badges for quick scan.

detail_page.ui_component: Rich text/markdown block. Use simple language and clear headings.
detail_page.notes: Answers the fundamental 'what' and 'why'. Crucial for understanding.

detail_page.ui_component: Combination of prominent bullet points/chips for key criteria (e.g., 'Age 18-60', 'For Women', 'SC/ST'), followed by detailed markdown `eligibility_md`. Potentially a mini 'check your eligibility' widget.
detail_page.notes: Most critical section for users to self-identify if they qualify. Simplify complex rules.

detail_page.ui_component: Clear headings for each benefit, use `benefits_md` for rich details. Display `benefit_amount_inr` or `benefit_amount_percent` prominently with currency/percentage. Visual icons for benefit types.
detail_page.notes: Directly addresses the user's primary motivation. Use simple, direct language for amounts and types.

detail_page.display_logic: Always. If `how_to_apply` (guide) exists, use its structure. Else, use `application_process_md`. Always show `apply_url`.
detail_page.ui_component: Step-by-step visual flow (numbered cards or infographic) if `how_to_apply` guide exists. Otherwise, a clear, numbered list from `application_process_md`. Prominent 'Apply Now' button (`apply_url`) at the end.
detail_page.notes: Simplify the application process into actionable, easy-to-follow steps. Crucial for low-literacy users.

detail_page.ui_component: Simple bullet list. Consider icons for common documents (Aadhar, Photo).
detail_page.notes: Helps users prepare before starting the application, reducing frustration.

detail_page.display_logic: Only if `faqs` array is not empty.
detail_page.ui_component: Accordion/collapsible list for each Q&A pair.
detail_page.notes: Addresses typical user concerns, reduces support queries. Questions and answers should be very simple.

detail_page.display_logic: Only if `use_case` (Hindi guide) exists (will use generated English version).
detail_page.ui_component: Testimonial card with beneficiary `name`, `age`, `occupation`, `state`, and `story`. Use a warm, inviting design.
detail_page.notes: Builds trust and provides relatable context, inspiring potential beneficiaries. Will need to be generated/translated.

detail_page.display_logic: Only if `tips` or `common_mistakes` (Hindi guide) exist (will use generated English versions).
detail_page.ui_component: Two distinct bulleted lists, potentially with encouraging/cautionary icons. Clearly labelled.
detail_page.notes: Practical advice to ensure successful application and avoid common pitfalls. Will need to be generated/translated.
Important for clarity, explicitly stating who cannot apply. Could be integrated into Eligibility but keep separate due to distinct data field.

detail_page.ui_component: Collapsible section or 'More Info' tab. List `category[]` and `tags[]` as chips. List `ministry`, `nodal_department` as key-value pairs. Display `references_json` as clickable links with titles.
detail_page.notes: For users seeking deeper information or official sources. Essential for transparency and trust. Include data attribution.

content_gaps.prompt_hint: Translate the Hindi `how_to_apply` steps into simple, actionable English. If Hindi is missing, extract step-by-step instructions from `application_process_md`. Focus on clarity for low-literacy users, using direct verbs. Include any relevant URLs from `apply_url` within steps.
content_gaps.why_valuable: Crucial for non-Hindi speaking users to understand application process before full 22-language translation. Improves accessibility and reduces application barriers.

content_gaps.prompt_hint: Translate the Hindi `use_case` story into simple English (50-100 words). If Hindi is missing, generate a short, relatable real-life success story of a beneficiary, including their `name` (generic, e.g., 'Ramesh from Uttar Pradesh'), `age`, `occupation`, and `state`, demonstrating how they benefited from the scheme. Focus on the scheme's main purpose and impact.
content_gaps.why_valuable: Builds trust and helps users visualize the impact and relevance of the scheme in their own lives. Emotional connection is powerful.

content_gaps.prompt_hint: Translate the Hindi `tips` into simple English. If Hindi is missing, generate 3-5 practical, easy-to-understand tips for successfully applying for or benefiting from this scheme, based on its `eligibility_md`, `documents_required_md`, and `application_process_md`.
content_gaps.why_valuable: Provides actionable guidance, improving the chances of a successful application and maximizing benefits for the user.

content_gaps.prompt_hint: Translate the Hindi `common_mistakes` into simple English. If Hindi is missing, generate 3-5 common mistakes applicants might make when applying for this scheme and clear advice on how to avoid them. Draw from `eligibility_md`, `documents_required_md`, `application_process_md`, and `exclusions_md`.
content_gaps.why_valuable: Prevents frustration, saves time, and reduces application rejections by proactively addressing potential errors.

content_gaps.prompt_hint: Generate 3-4 ultra-short bullet points (5-10 words each) summarizing the scheme's main purpose, key eligibility, and primary benefit. Use extremely simple vocabulary suitable for a low-literacy audience.
content_gaps.why_valuable: Improves scannability and quick comprehension for users browsing lists or seeking quick answers. Excellent for meta descriptions and quick insights on scheme cards.

content_gaps.prompt_hint: Generate 3-5 simple 'Yes/No' questions from the scheme's primary eligibility criteria. Prioritize criteria that are easy to answer and exclude a large number of ineligible users quickly (e.g., 'Are you a woman?', 'Is your age between X and Y?', 'Do you belong to a Scheduled Caste or Tribe?').
content_gaps.why_valuable: Enhances the 'Who can benefit?' section with an interactive element, making self-assessment faster and more intuitive for low-literacy users.

home_page.ui_component: Prominent search bar, followed by a 'Quick Eligibility Check' button that navigates to `/check` or opens a mini-form modal. Suggest popular search queries.
home_page.notes: Main entry point; make search and eligibility checking highly visible and simple.

home_page.ui_component: Horizontal carousel of scheme cards (using `scheme_card` spec) based on internal analytics or admin curation.
home_page.notes: Helps users discover high-demand schemes easily. Keep descriptions brief and engaging.

home_page.ui_component: List or carousel of scheme cards. Requires a `created_at` or `updated_at` field in the database for tracking.
home_page.notes: Keeps the portal feeling fresh and encourages repeat visits. Highlights recent changes or additions.

home_page.ui_component: Section displaying a selection of scheme cards (using `scheme_card` spec) filtered by user's detected/selected state (for `level='State'` and `level='Central'` schemes). Requires state detection or selection mechanism.
home_page.notes: Personalizes the experience, making relevant schemes immediately visible. Critical for rural users.

home_page.ui_component: A few prominent buttons or illustrative cards for the top 3-5 most frequently accessed `category[]` pages (e.g., 'Farmers', 'Women', 'Students', 'Health').
home_page.notes: Provides direct, visual access to high-demand categories, simplifying navigation for users with specific needs.

home_page.ui_component: Grid of all `category[]` cards with icons and short labels.
home_page.notes: Comprehensive overview of all scheme classifications, aiding structured discovery.

browse_filters.ui_component: Multi-select checkboxes (Male, Female, All Genders, Other).
Dual-handle range slider with input fields (e.g., 'From Age', 'To Age').
Input field for 'Max Income (INR)' or range slider with common thresholds. For simplicity, 'Income less than X lakhs'.
Multi-select checkboxes/chips (SC, ST, OBC, General, Minority).
Multi-select checkboxes/chips (Cash, Scholarship, Loan, In-kind, Service).
Dropdown with search functionality (potentially in a 'More Filters' section due to length).

browse_filters.field: for_married, for_widow, for_pregnant, for_shg, for_girl_child, for_sc_st, for_minority, disability_required
browse_filters.ui_component: Grouped checkboxes under a 'Special Conditions' heading (e.g., 'For Married Individuals', 'For Widows', 'For Persons with Disabilities').

full_text_fields.strategy: Exact match, partial match, typo tolerance
Exact match, partial match, typo tolerance
Full-text search, semantic relevance, synonym mapping
Full-text search, semantic relevance, synonym mapping
Exact match of category names, synonym mapping
Exact match of tag names, synonym mapping
Exact match of type, synonym mapping

strategies: Typo Tolerance/Fuzzy Matching (e.g., PostgreSQL `pg_trgm` extension for trigram similarity)
Relevance Ranking (boost exact matches in name/short_title, then description/benefits/eligibility, then categories/tags)
Stemming and Stop Word Removal (standard NLP for English text)
Snippet Highlighting (show where the search term appears in the result description)
Integrated Filtering (allow users to apply `browse_filters` after initial search results)

page_types_to_generate.purpose: Primary landing page for users searching for specific schemes or deep dives into a scheme.
Aggregation page for users searching for schemes related to a specific need (e.g., 'farmer schemes').
Aggregation page for users searching for schemes available in their region.
Aggregation page for users searching for schemes by administrative level.
While dynamic, ensure shareable URLs and potentially meta-descriptions for matched schemes to allow for some indexability of specific eligibility scenarios. Use canonicals if needed.

properties: Standard breadcrumb structure (Home > Category > Scheme Name).

schema_markup_recommendations.applies_to: Home, About, Browse, Category, State, Level pages

templates.Category Page: Government Schemes for {{category}} in India | Aarambha Haq
templates.Home Page: Aarambha Haq: Discover All Indian Government Welfare Schemes in 22 Languages

templates.Scheme Detail: {{short_summary_bullet_points}}... Apply online on Aarambha Haq. Get eligibility, benefits, documents & application details.
templates.Category Page: Find all government welfare schemes related to {{category}} for Indian citizens. Explore eligibility, benefits & how to apply easily on Aarambha Haq.
templates.State Page: Discover central and state government schemes for citizens of {{state}}. Get details on eligibility, benefits, and application process on Aarambha Haq.
templates.Home Page: Aarambha Haq helps you find, understand, and apply for 2700+ Indian government welfare schemes in 22 languages. Check eligibility & apply easily.

templates.og:title: Same as Meta Title
templates.og:image: Use `scheme_image_url` if available, else site logo.

fields_to_show.notes: Main heading, primary link to detail page.
Secondary title, provides quick context, below `name`.
A very brief 1-2 sentence summary. Ideal if `short_summary_bullet_points` is generated.
Badge 'DBT' if true, signifying direct transfer.
1-2 most defining eligibility chips (e.g., 'For Women', 'Age 18-60', 'For SC/ST', 'For PwD'). Choose dynamically based on scheme specifics or pre-defined hierarchy.
Concise benefit indicator (e.g., 'Cash Benefit', 'Scholarship', 'Loan up to ₹1 Lakh').
Prominent 'Apply Now' button, direct call to action.
